2606.21613.title: Cross-Modal Corroboration for Annotation-Free Wildlife Monitoring
2606.21613.paper_summary: Scaling wildlife monitoring for real-world conservation deployments requires automated analysis of smart sensors that operate under severe annotation scarcity. We propose leveraging expert knowledge of species activity patterns as an annotation-free validation signal for multimodal monitoring pipelines. We operationalize agreement as the alignment of independently derived hourly activity curves both with each other and with published behavioral priors-a three-way convergence that rules out shared-data confounds and dataset-internal correlation as alternative explanations. Our vision pipeline combines zero-shot species detection via BioCLIP 2, sliced inference to handle deployment-constrained camera positioning, and geometry-based geographic localization from camera trap imagery. Our acoustic pipeline detects species vocalizations via a fine-tuned classifier. We validate the pipeline on a breeding herd of Milu deer and demonstrate that both modalities independently recover activity patterns consistent with known deer behavioral ecology with minimal manual annotation. The framework applies to species detectable in both visual and acoustic modalities for which behavioral priors are documented in the literature, suggesting a practical path toward self-validating wildlife-monitoring pipelines at conservation scale.
2606.21613.paper_summary_zh: 擴大野生動物監測以應對現實世界的保護部署，需要對在嚴重標註稀缺的情況下運作的智能傳感器進行自動化分析。我們建議利用專家對物種活動模式的知識作為無需標註的驗證信號，來支持多模態監測管道。我們將一致性操作化為獨立推導的每小時活動曲線之間的對齊，這些曲線不僅彼此對齊，還與已發表的行為先驗相一致——這是一種三方收斂，排除了共享數據混淆和數據集內部相關性作為替代解釋。我們的視覺管道結合了通過 BioCLIP 2 的零樣本物種檢測、切片推理以處理受部署限制的相機定位，以及來自相機陷阱圖像的基於幾何的地理定位。我們的聲學管道通過經過微調的分類器檢測物種的聲音。我們在一群繁殖的梅鹿上驗證了該管道，並展示了這兩種模態獨立地恢復了與已知鹿類行為生態一致的活動模式，並且幾乎不需要手動標註。該框架適用於在文獻中有行為先驗記錄的可在視覺和聲學模態中檢測的物種，這表明了一條實用的道路，朝著在保護規模上自我驗證的野生動物監測管道邁進。
2606.21613.author: Bharath Pillai et.al.
2606.21613.authors: Bharath Pillai, Varun Viswapriyan, Christopher Stewart, Tanya Berger-Wolf, Jenna Kline
